verdict_reason: The traditional meaning is that vyavasāyātmikā buddhi is the resolved-by-prior-determination intelligence which is the precondition for buddhi-yoga; many-branched is the diagnostic of irresolution rooted in skipped groundwork. The engineering layer's central claim — 'branching past where evidence should collapse it is the diagnostic of irresolution; resolute practitioners branch once and stop' — matches the Sanskrit and both commentaries (Shankara: right pramāṇa anchoring; Ramanuja: prior ātma-yāthātmya determination). The reverse-translation test: 'a settled intellect anchored in customer-need and system-truth, contrasted with a many-branched mind whose proliferation diagnoses skipped grounding' translates back to vyavasāyātmikā buddhi (anchored conviction) vs bahu-śākhā buddhi of avyavasāyinām (proliferating intelligences of the irresolute). No distortion. PASS for both 3.6 (distortion test) and 7.10 (reverse-translate compatibility).
